column12: Optional Clarification

column6: Bill Of Lading/Master Bill Of Lading

column12: Master Bill of Lading is mandatory for sea shipments. House Bill of Lading is required only if cargo is consolidated by a freight forwarder/NVOCC.
Used for advance payments and initial quotes. Customs processing requires the final Commercial Invoice.

column6: Bill Of Entry/Bill Of Entry (Assessed Copy)

column12: Optional. Requirement is dictated entirely by agreed Incoterms (Mandatory for CIF/CIP; Not required for FOB/EXW).
Optional. Dependent on commodity type, buyer terms, and destination regulations. COA is typical for chemicals/food.
Optional.  COO is used for preferential duty rates under Free Trade Agreements.
Optional. Used to claim priority sector lending, lower statutory fees, or specific export incentives (like subsidies or subvention schemes).
Optional. A trade facilitation certification providing benefits like faster clearance, reduced physical inspections, and deferred duty payments.

Optional for exports, not required for imports. Required specifically if you intend to export goods without the upfront payment of IGST. If IGST is paid with a subsequent claim for refund, an LUT is not required.
Optional for imports, not required for exports. Submitted to customs to affix mandated statutory labels (MRP, importer details) in a bonded warehouse before final clearance.
Mandatory only if the commodity is a food item or food-contact material.
Mandatory only if the commodity is a food item or food-contact material.
Mandatory only if the cargo utilizes solid wood packaging materials (e.g., wooden pallets) to comply with ISPM 15 standards, or for specific agricultural commodities.
Plant Quarantine NOC is required by import customs to clear incoming agricultural goods.
A Phytosanitary Certificate is issued by the exporting country to certify the goods are free of pests; it is required for exports.
Optional status. Units under this scheme are permitted to import raw materials and capital goods duty-free, subject to achieving positive net foreign exchange earnings.
Optional. EPCG allows duty-free import of machinery for production.
Optional. Advance Authorization allows duty-free import of raw materials required to manufacture export goods.
Optional. Required to claim export incentives under the Foreign Trade Policy.
Mandatory only for exporting scheduled agricultural and processed food products from India.
Optional. Allows exporters to stuff and seal containers at their own factory premises rather than undergoing physical inspection at the port/CFS.
Mandatory post-shipment export document issued by the bank confirming receipt of foreign exchange.
Statutory declarations (like EDF for exports) required by the Reserve Bank of India to monitor foreign exchange flows.
Solely dependent on the negotiated financial terms between buyer and seller.
Mandatory for Sea Export. Dictated by SOLAS (Safety of Life at Sea) regulations to declare exact container weight prior to vessel loading.
Issued by the carrier/forwarder to the consignee advising that the imported cargo is arriving.
Triggered strictly by the chemical composition or appearance of the cargo.
Triggered strictly by the chemical composition or appearance of the cargo. Non-DG is heavily utilized in Air Export to confirm harmlessness.
Mandatory for importing e-waste, plastics, and batteries.
Mandatory for pre-packaged retail commodities.
Mandatory for equipment utilizing wireless/Bluetooth frequencies.
Highly specific to pharmaceuticals/cosmetics, radioactive/medical equipment, wildlife flora/fauna, and live animals.
Mandatory for importing items like unshredded metal scrap to certify absence of explosives/radioactivity.
Highly specific to pharmaceuticals/cosmetics, radioactive/medical equipment, wildlife flora/fauna, and live animals.
Highly specific to pharmaceuticals/cosmetics, radioactive/medical equipment, wildlife flora/fauna, and live animals.
Highly specific to pharmaceuticals/cosmetics, radioactive/medical equipment, wildlife flora/fauna, and live animals.
Highly specific to pharmaceuticals/cosmetics, radioactive/medical equipment, wildlife flora/fauna, and live animals.
Often required by importing countries to ensure the exported medical/food product is legally sold in the origin country.
Globally mandatory specifically for the trade of rough diamonds to prevent conflict diamond circulation.
Optional export incentive documents generated to claim duty credits.
Dependent entirely on the importing country's customs requirements.
